published_prior_art.cite: LuShiFangZhangDing2025BipartiteDesign gives the homogeneous Bernoulli bipartite Hajek variance formula and CLT; arXiv:2501.09844

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry has a bipartite heterogeneous-Bernoulli Hajek variance envelope; exp_design_pm1_exactness_boundary_v1 is a one-mode covariance-SDP implementability problem.

published_prior_art.cite: LuShiFangZhangDing2025BipartiteDesign treats common Bernoulli(p) and explicitly leaves Bernoulli(p_k) extensions to future work; HarshawSavjeEisenstatMirrokniPougetAbadie2021BipartiteERL is under a linear exposure-response model.

repo_prior_art.cite: exp_design_pm1_exactness_boundary_v1 optimizes implementable covariance geometry, not heterogeneous intervention-unit Bernoulli probabilities for a bipartite Hajek estimator.

published_prior_art.cite: Viviano2026NetworkDesign optimizes pilot-estimated variance for one-mode two-wave network experiments; no checked paper solves this graph-only bipartite minimax-p problem.

repo_prior_art.cite: exp_ego_cluster_exposure_clt was downgraded because routing exposure bookkeeping into a dependency-graph CLT is subfield unless paired with a stronger design object.

published_prior_art.cite: LuShiFangZhangDing2025BipartiteDesign proves the homogeneous bipartite Hajek CLT; Leung2019ApproxNeighborhoodInterference proves IPW normality under approximate neighborhood interference.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry gives post-design conservative variance for graph-optimized heterogeneous bipartite Hajek assignment.

published_prior_art.cite: LuShiFangZhangDing2025BipartiteDesign gives homogeneous conservative variance; HarshawMiddletonSavje2021OptimizedVariance and ParkWager2026NeymanJackknife give adjacent general conservative-variance frameworks.

repo_prior_art.cite: exp_design_pm1_exactness_boundary_v1 leaves an exact phase frontier for a different covariance-implementability loss, not this reciprocal-product Bernoulli surrogate certificate.

published_prior_art.cite: UganderKarrerBackstromKleinberg2013GraphClusterRandomization and UganderYin2020RandomizedGCR give graph-cluster variance controls, not a constant-factor certificate for heterogeneous bipartite Bernoulli minimax design.

soundness_flags.label: prop:homogeneous-reduction / thm:hetero-envelope
soundness_flags.one_line: The cross-arm sign does not reduce to Lu et al.'s homogeneous formula: with r_{ij}^{10}=-1 on overlap, the covariance expansion needs the opposite sign from the displayed +2 r_{ij}^{10} term.

soundness_flags.label: thm:hetero-clt
soundness_flags.one_line: v_{G_n,p}(Y)=n^{-2} sum E[xi_i xi_j] is the variance scale of n^{-1} sum xi_i, so sqrt(n){hat_tau_H-tau_n}/sqrt(v_{G_n,p}) has the wrong normalization.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redraft the first-order variance algebra with a single cross-arm sign convention and explicitly verify the p_k=p reduction against Lu et al.'s Lambda_1, Lambda_0, Lambda_tau formula before reintroducing the envelope.
Fix the CLT scaling by either redefining v_{G_n,p} as n times the estimator variance or changing the standardized statistic to divide hat_tau_H-tau_n by sqrt(v_{G_n,p}).

After the sign fix, state whether V_env is an exact supremum over centered bounded potential-outcome vectors or only a conservative graph-only upper envelope; if exact, include the centering constraints in the optimization.
Add a small worked graph, such as two outcome units sharing one intervention unit, computing r^1, r^0, the cross block, V_env, and the KKT equations from primitives.

recommended_next_step: Reject the current core as algebraically unsound; redraft the variance sign and CLT normalization, then resubmit the same field-tier angle.
